samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable bit for multiplication. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable bit for multiplication. Product. MUL_WIDTH is the width of multiplier and multiplicand.

Reset the internal registers.

if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 1'b0; mul_a_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; mul_b_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; end

Update the mul_en_out signal based on the mul_en_in signal.

// Update mul_en_out_reg if (clk) begin mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_in, mul_en_out_reg[MUL_WIDTH-1:1]}; end mul_en_out <= mul_en_out_reg[MUL_WIDTH];

Update the internal multiplicand and multiplier registers.

// Update mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg if (clk && mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end

Generate the partial products based on the multiplier bits.

// Generate partial products for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH; i++) begin if (mul_b_reg[i]) begin temp[i] <= mul_a_reg; end else begin temp[i] <= 0; end end

Calculate the partial sums based on the generated partial products.

// Calculate partial sums for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH-1; i++) begin if (i == 0) begin sum[i] <= temp[i]; end else begin sum[i] <= sum[i-1] + temp[i]; end end

Calculate the final product based on the last partial sum.

// Calculate final product if (clk && mul_en_in) begin mul_out_reg <= sum[MUL_WIDTH-2]; end

Assign the output based on the enable signal.

// Assign output if (mul_en_out) begin mul_out <= mul_out_reg; end else begin mul_out <= 0; end
